\chapter{Concordance with primary literature}
\label{chap:concordance}

\index{concordance|textbf}

This chapter is the constitution of the monograph. When chapters
disagree, this chapter governs. It records the five main theorems
with their precise status and locates every result against the
prior literature: what is proved, what is conditional, what
remains conjectural.

The governing principle is falsification: every claim in these three
volumes is presumed false until it appears below with an explicit
status tag and a proof reference or independent verification trail.
A theorem that contradicts the concordance is not a theorem; it is an
error in the chapter that states it. Omission from this chapter is
itself a verdict: what is not recorded here has not survived scrutiny.
The concordance does not summarize the monograph; the monograph
approximates the concordance.

chapters/examples/w-algebras-stable.tex:4:W-algebras are the decisive test cases for the $A_\infty$ chiral framework in the $d' = 1$ regime. The reason is structural: the pre-reduction affine vertex algebra $V_k(\mathfrak{g})$ is Koszul (Theorem~\ref{thm:one-loop-koszul}), so the transferred $A_\infty$ operations on bar cohomology vanish ($m_k = 0$ for $k \ge 3$), though the Swiss-cheese operations on $V_k(\mathfrak{g})$ itself have $m_3^{\mathrm{SC}} \neq 0$ (class~$\mathbf{L}$, depth~$3$). Drinfeld--Sokolov reduction changes this. The BRST functor manufactures the higher operations $m_k$ for $k \ge 3$ that break Swiss-cheese formality (Theorem~\ref{thm:ds-koszul-obstruction}): the quartic OPE pole of the Virasoro generator, the quintic pole of $W_3$, and the higher poles of $W_N$ are all artefacts of the reduction, absent in the affine input. The resulting $\mathcal{W}$-algebra remains chirally Koszul (the bar complex is well-behaved), but the $A_\infty$ structure is genuinely infinite. This section computes the $A_\infty$ operations, spectral $r$-matrices, and deformation data for Virasoro and $W_3$ via the Khan--Zeng 3D holomorphic-topological Poisson sigma model \cite{KZ25}.

# Vol II Cross-Volume Propagation

This skill exists to stop local truth from coexisting with global drift.

- active Vol II chapter files
- active Vol II appendices
- superseded split files that still advertise the same claim
- `~/chiral-bar-cobar`
- `~/calabi-yau-quantum-groups` when the bridge is genuinely cross-volume
- `compute/` and `compute/tests/`

FRONTIER.md:162:## F9. E_1 Verdier on Ordered Configurations
FRONTIER.md:166:**The physics**: Verdier duality on the Ran space intertwines B(A) and B(A!) — it is the algebraic incarnation of electric-magnetic / S-duality in the HT theory. The ordered bar B^ord lives on Conf^<(X), not Ran(X). A naive D_Ran(B^ord) doesn't exist: pushing forward to Ran loses the ordering.
FRONTIER.md:168:**The correct E_1 analogue**: Opposite-duality B^ord(A^op) = B^ord(A)^cop. The two-colour double Koszul duality theorem (thm:two-color-master) confirms: closed colour uses Verdier/Ran; open colour uses LINEAR duality.
FRONTIER.md:170:**What would be needed**: D_{Conf^<} (Verdier duality on ordered configuration spaces) or a ribbon Ran space. This is a genuine open direction in higher algebra.
FRONTIER.md:303:11. Ordered Verdier doesn't exist; opposite-duality is the E₁ analogue

chapters/theory/introduction.tex-142-
chapters/theory/introduction.tex:143:The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: it is the scalar such that $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.

\begin{theorem}[Koszul comparison; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{thm:koszul-comparison-mc}
\index{Koszul comparison!MC coupling|textbf}
If $\cA$ is chirally Koszul
\textup{(}bar cohomology concentrated in bar degree~$1$;
any of the $10$ unconditional equivalent conditions of
Theorem~\textup{\ref{V1-thm:koszul-equivalences-meta}}\textup{)},
then the Koszul dual $\cA^!$ is well-defined and the
comparison functor
\begin{align*}
 \Phi \colon \operatorname{Line}(\cA)
 &\;\longrightarrow\; \Perf(\cA^!) \\
 (V, \mu) &\;\longmapsto\;
 (V,\, \rho = (\mathrm{id}_V \otimes \pi)(\mu))
\end{align*}
is a quasi-equivalence, where
$\pi \colon \cA \twoheadrightarrow \cA^!$ is the bar-cohomology

   180	$C_\ast(W(\SCchtop))$. The bar complex

   185	
   186	\textbf{Step 2} (Cofree rigidity).
   187	On a cofree conilpotent coalgebra $T^c(V)$, every coderivation
   188	$D$ is uniquely determined by the composite
   189	$\pi_1 \circ D \colon T^c(V) \to V$ (the cogenerator projection).
   190	This is a standard fact of coalgebra theory
   191	\textup{(}Volume~I, Proposition~\ref*{prop:cofree-coderivation}\textup{)}.
   192	In particular, $d_{\barB}$ is the unique coderivation whose
   193	projection to cogenerators in degree~$k$ is the residue map
   194	\[
   195	\Res_{D_{1\cdots k}} \colon
   196	\Omega^\bullet_{\log}(\FM_k(\C)) \otimes \cA^{\otimes k}
   197	\;\longrightarrow\; \cA
   198	\]
   199	along the total collision divisor in $\FM_k(\C)$.

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '130,170p' chapters/theory/introduction.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2 succeeded in 52ms:
Koszul model for a Lagrangian self-intersection
$\cL \times_{\cM}^h \cL$ in a $(-2)$-shifted symplectic stack
$\cM$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-is-self-intersection}). The
differential is the Koszul resolution of the diagonal, and the
coproduct is the groupoid diagonal of the self-intersection.
The chiral algebra determines a formal local bulk model: the
boundary fixes the shifted-cotangent side of the bulk
reconstruction (Theorem~\ref{thm:holographic-reconstruction}),
but recovering the actual formal neighborhood requires the
formal Darboux theorem for $(-2)$-shifted symplectic stacks.
The holographic principle, on this surface, is the Darboux
theorem.

The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: it is the scalar such that $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.

\emph{Lines (abstract).}
Theorem~\ref{thm:lines_as_modules} provides the abstract line-side
package: the open-colour $E_1$-module category associated to the
bar complex $\barB(\mathrm{Vir}_c)$. This exists for any

no additional input beyond homotopy-Koszulity of the operad
(Theorem~\ref{thm:homotopy-Koszul}).

\subsection{The M2-brane instanton particle: bar-cobar as holographic duality}
\label{subsec:M2-holography}

Costello's M2-brane program~\cite{Costello-1705.02500v1,costello-gaiotto}
identifies, at large rank~$K$, the M2-brane boundary algebra
as Koszul dual to the 11d twisted supergravity bulk algebra.

\begin{remark}[Mirror symmetry as Koszul duality]
\label{rem:mirror-as-koszul}
This mirror symmetry is a bar-cobar equivalence.  Both theories
have equivalent bar complexes: their dg coalgebras
$\bar{B}(\cA_{\partial}^{\mathrm{SQED}})$ and
$\bar{B}(\cA_{\partial}^{\mathrm{XYZ}})$ are quasi-isomorphic. So
Theorem~B (bar-cobar inversion) of Volume~I produces the VOA
isomorphism.  Mirror symmetry is not an accident: it is Koszul
duality.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Exchange of boundary conditions]
\label{rem:mirror-bc-exchange}
Under the SQED--XYZ duality, boundary conditions exchange:
Neumann~$\leftrightarrow$~Dirichlet.  In the bar-cobar framework
this is the exchange $\cA \leftrightarrow \cA^!$; Koszul duality
exchanges the two sides of the bar-cobar adjunction
(Theorem~A of Volume~I).  The Neumann algebra of one theory is the
Dirichlet algebra of the mirror, and the exchange is encoded on
 succeeded in 51ms:
operators form a commutative algebra; boundary operators need not
commute; line operators carry a monoidal structure from fusion.
The deficiency is structural: the three operator classes live at
three different categorical levels. What corrects it?

The answer is forced by the geometry of the slab. Place $T$ on
$\mathbb{C}_z \times I$, where $I = [0,1]$ is an interval with
a boundary condition $D$ on $\{0\}$ and a transverse boundary
condition $N$ on $\{1\}$. Each face supports a chiral algebra:
$\cA$ on the $D$-wall, $\cA^!$ on the $N$-wall
(Volume~I, Theorem~A identifies $\cA^!$ as the Koszul dual).
The slab $D \,|\, T \,|\, N$ has \emph{two} boundary
components \cite{Dimofte25}. It is a bimodule geometry, not a
Swiss-cheese disk: the Swiss-cheese operad $\SCchtop$ has one
closed interior boundary and one open boundary, while the slab
has two walls of the same type, related by transversality.

A line operator $\ell$ threaded through the slab defines a vector
space

Identify:

- the exact target statement or surface;
- labels, hypotheses, and dependencies;
- whether the task is question-answering, audit, rectification, formula verification, build triage, or frontier synthesis.

- lambda-bracket versus OPE-mode conventions;
- genus/degree/filtration/family scope;

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '110,160p' chapters/theory/introduction.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2 succeeded in 52ms:

Three objects are independently constructed from~$\cC$. The
\emph{universal bulk} is the chiral derived center
$\cZ^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cC) =
\RHom_{\operatorname{Fun}(\cC,\cC)}(\operatorname{Id},
\operatorname{Id})$,
computed in any chart by chiral Hochschild cochains
$C^\bullet_{\mathrm{ch}}(A_b, A_b)$. The \emph{line-side/open-colour
Koszul dual algebra}~$A^!_{\mathrm{line}}$ is extracted from the
Verdier dual $D_{\Ran}(\barB(A_b)) \simeq \barB(A_b^!)$
(Theorem~A intertwining): the Verdier dual of the bar coalgebra is
the bar of the Koszul dual algebra, and on the Koszul locus its
cohomology recovers~$A^!_{\mathrm{line}}$. The \emph{twisting morphism
classifier} is the bar complex itself, $\barB(A_b)$, which
represents universal couplings between~$A_b$ and~$A_b^!$ via
Volume~I's Theorem~A (the bar-cobar adjunction and Verdier intertwining). These are three different objects produced by
three different functors, solving three different problems:
the bar complex is \emph{not} the bulk.

The bar complex carries a geometric interpretation as a formal-local

\begin{theorem}[Boundary BRST recovery for twisted Yang--Mills data; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
\label{thm:twisted-ym-boundary-brst}
Let $(X,\mathcal{T},\cB)$ be a Koszul-admissible twisted Yang--Mills boundary datum.
Then:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
\item there is a quasi-isomorphism
\[
\mathsf{Open}_{\cB} = \barBch(\cA_{\cB}) \simeq C^{\frac{\infty}{2}+\bullet}(\cA_{\cB}),
\]
identifying the chiral bar complex with the open-string BRST complex of the boundary condition;
\item Verdier duality converts the open sector into a factorization \emph{algebra}:
\[
\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\, \mathsf{Open}_{\cB} \simeq \mathsf{Bulk}^{\vee}_{\cB} \simeq (\cA_{\cB}^!)_\infty.
\]

\bigskip

These seven results are coordinates on a single object,
not independent: each determines the others
through the Stasheff identities, the Koszul pairing, and the
genus-tower recursion. The rigidity has a geometric source.

\section*{Movement I\@: The single principle}

\providecommand{\cL}{\mathcal{L}}
\providecommand{\Mvac}{\cM_{\mathrm{vac}}}
\providecommand{\Mbar}{\overline{\mathcal M}}

The categorical logarithm of Volume~I is a Lagrangian embedding.
The bar construction $\barB^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ maps a chiral
algebra~$\cA$ into the derived moduli~$\Mvac$ of local bulk vacua,
and the image is a shifted Lagrangian
$\cL_\cA \hookrightarrow \Mvac$: the $(-2)$-shifted symplectic
structure on $\Mvac$

\item \textbf{Chiral Koszulness from physics}: (H1)--(H4) + BV-BRST should imply chiral Koszulness for the resulting algebras. The monograph warns this is non-trivial (chiral $\neq$ classical Koszulness). \emph{Status: Open.}

\item \textbf{Higher-genus $A_\infty$ structure}: This paper works on $\C \times \R$ (genus 0 in $\C$). The monograph's genus tower should lift to $A_\infty$ with spectral parameters. \emph{Status: Analytic lane of MC5 proved at all genera; $D^{\mathrm{co}}$-level BV=bar proved for all shadow classes; genuswise chain-level BV/BRST/bar identification conjectural (class~M chain-level false).} The genus-$1$ spectral braiding is treated by the elliptic spectral dichotomy (Theorem~\ref{thm:elliptic-spectral-dichotomy}): the Coisson bracket $c_0 = \{a{}_0 b\}$ controls whether curvature and braiding decouple (Cartan type) or entangle (Yangian type). At genus~$g \geq 2$, the Feynman-diagrammatic identification with the algebraic bar differential is established by inductive genus determination (Volume~I, Theorem~\ref*{V1-thm:inductive-genus-determination}) and the analytic-algebraic comparison (Volume~I, Theorem~\ref*{V1-thm:analytic-algebraic-comparison}), subject to the conjectural genuswise chain-level BV/BRST/bar identification ($D^{\mathrm{co}}$-level proved).

\item \textbf{Spectral braiding beyond evaluation}: Can $R(z)$ from \S\ref{sec:spectral_braiding} be promoted from the evaluation locus to the full factorization category? The all-types evaluation-generated-core DK comparison is proved, but the post-core extension beyond that locus remains open.

\item \textbf{Curvature-braiding coupling}: \emph{Resolved} (Theorem~\ref{thm:elliptic-spectral-dichotomy}). The pole structure of the $\lambda$-bracket determines whether genus-$1$ curvature and spectral braiding interact: the Coisson bracket $c_0 = \{a{}_0 b\}$ is the discriminant. Vanishing $c_0$ (Heisenberg, lattice Cartan) gives decoupling; non-vanishing $c_0$ (affine KM, Virasoro, $W$-algebras) gives entanglement through the shared $B$-cycle quasi-periodicity of the Weierstrass zeta function.
\end{enumerate}

At genus~$1$, replace the rational propagator by the elliptic
propagator $\eta_{ij}^{(1)} = d\log\sigma(z_i - z_j \mid \tau)$,
where $\sigma$ is the Weierstrass sigma function. The
quasi-periodicity of the Weierstrass $\zeta$-function
$\zeta = \sigma'/\sigma$ breaks the Arnold combination:
\begin{equation}
\label{eq:genus-1-arnold-defect}
 A_3^{(1)}
 \;=\;
 E_2(\tau) \cdot
 (dz_1 - dz_2) \wedge (dz_2 - dz_3),
\end{equation}
where $E_2(\tau) = 1 - 24\sum_{n \geq 1} \sigma_1(n)\,q^n$
is the quasi-modular Eisenstein series of weight~$2$.
The genus-$1$ bar curvature is therefore
\begin{equation}
\label{eq:genus-1-bar-curvature}
 d_{\barB}^2\big|_{g=1}

The example also illustrates the three-functor distinction
(bar $\neq$ Verdier dual $\neq$ cobar):
\begin{itemize}[nosep]
\item $\barB(V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2))$: the bar complex,
  a factorization coalgebra classifying twisting morphisms.
\item $\Omega(\barB(V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2))) \simeq
  V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$: bar-cobar inversion recovers the
  boundary algebra (Theorem~B).
\item $\mathbb{D}_{\mathrm{Ran}}(\barB(V_k(\mathfrak{sl}_2)))
  \simeq \barB(V_{-k-4}(\mathfrak{sl}_2))$: Verdier duality
  gives the bar of the Koszul dual (Theorem~A).
\item $C^\bullet_{\mathrm{ch}}(V_k, V_k)$: the derived center,
  a \emph{fourth} object, gives the universal bulk.
\end{itemize}

the derived fiber product that carries a $(-1)$-shifted symplectic

\section{The Drinfeld double programme, part (a): $E_1$-chiral construction}
\label{sec:drinfeld-double-e1}

\subsection*{The unifying thesis and the four consistency conditions}

The object of the Drinfeld double programme is a single thesis.
The boundary algebra $\cA$ determines the bulk via its derived
chiral centre $Z^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$; the level
$\kappa(\cA)$ is the holographic central charge; the shadow
tower $\{\Theta_\cA^{(n)}\}$ is the hierarchy of holographic
corrections; and the chiral double $U_\cA = \cA \bowtie \cA^!$
is the universal boundary-bulk reconstructor. Under this
thesis the four parts of the Drinfeld double programme are not
independent research problems. They are the four consistency
conditions that $U_\cA$ must satisfy to be the universal
reconstructor.

\begin{remark}[Programme-level meta-remark: four consistency conditions]
\label{rem:drinfeld-programme-four-conditions}
The four subproblems of the Drinfeld double programme are

\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item \emph{Layer~$1$: BV quantization of factorization
 algebras}~\cite{CG17}.
 Costello--Gwilliam construct perturbative QFT
 via factorization algebras on spacetime, with the BV formalism
 organized by the $E_\infty$-algebra structure on observables.
 In our framework this is
 Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main} at genus~$0$:
 bar-cobar adjunction for the boundary chiral algebra~$\cA_T$.
 The BV bracket on observables is the binary component of the
 modular convolution bracket on~$\gAmod$.

\item \emph{Layer~$2$: $4$d Chern--Simons and
 integrability}~\cite{CWY18}.
 Costello--Witten--Yamazaki show that $4$d holomorphic
 Chern--Simons theory on $\Sigma \times \mathbb{C}$ produces
 integrable lattice models, with the $R$-matrix arising from
 the holomorphic gauge field propagator. In our framework:
 the $R$-matrix is the binary genus-$0$ collision residue
 $r_T(z) = \operatorname{Res}^{\mathrm{coll}}_{0,2}(\Theta_{\cA_T})$

\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
Each vertex on the wheel has degree $n_i \geq 2$, with $2$ legs internal (connecting
to the two neighboring vertices on the loop) and $n_i - 2$ legs external. The total
number of external legs is $\sum_i (n_i - 2) = \sum_i n_i - 2k$. The constraint
$\sum n_i = 2k + e$ (where $e$ is the number of external legs) and the parity/support
constraints from the selection rule bound the number of allowed configurations. The
partition factor $p(k)$ counts the unordered multisets of degrees $(n_1, \ldots, n_k)$,
and the binomial factor counts the distinct ways to distribute the three lowest-mode
types among the external legs.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Convergence of the one-wheel sum]
% label removed: rem:one-wheel-convergence
The one-wheel sum $W_1 = \sum_{k \geq 1} W_1^{(k)}$ is a formal sum over
$k$-vertex wheel graphs. For the convergence of this sum in the completed
deformation complex, one needs the individual wheel amplitudes $W_1^{(k)}$ to
decrease sufficiently fast with $k$. The exceptional finiteness of the low-mode
sector (each vertex contributes a finite number of internal modes) ensures that

$\Theta_L$ on the genus-$0$ sector.
This is Vol~I Theorem~\ref{V1-thm:platonic-adjunction}
(adjunction) and Corollary~\ref{V1-cor:envelope-universal-mc}
(universal MC class).
\end{theorem}

\begin{remark}[Nishinaka and Vicedo]
% label removed: rem:thqg-nishinaka-vicedo
\index{factorization envelope!Nishinaka--Vicedo}
The genus-$0$ factorization envelope is constructed by Nishinaka
(2025/26) for Lie conformal algebras and by Vicedo (2025) in the
non-chiral setting. The modular extension to all genera is
proved in Vol~I via the bar-intrinsic construction
(Theorem~\ref*{V1-thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}) and the sewing machinery
of MC5 (Theorem~\ref{thm:general-hs-sewing}).
\end{remark}

\label{rem:E3-dichotomy-architecture}
\index{E3-algebra@$\Ethree$-algebra!dichotomy}
The passage from $\Ethree$-chiral to $\Ethree$-topological is
the volume's climax. For affine Kac--Moody at non-critical
level, this passage is a theorem
\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-km}\textup{)}.
For $\cW$-algebras obtained by \emph{any} DS reduction,
whether principal
\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-DS}\textup{)} or
non-principal
\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-DS-general}\textup{)},
including Virasoro, all $\cW_N$, and the Bershadsky--Polyakov
algebra, it is also a theorem:
the Costello--Gaiotto theorem provides the $3$d HT theory,
and the BRST identity
$T_{\mathrm{DS}} = [Q_{\mathrm{CS}}, G'_f]$ is verified by
constructing the DS-transported antighost~$G'_f$ from the
Sugawara antighost plus an improvement correction built from
derivatives of the $3$d BV antighosts.
Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} shows that the

\begin{proof}
Stages~$0$--$2$ are classical; see Loday--Vallette \cite{LV12},
Chapters~2, 9, and~11.
Stage~$3$: the BD chiral operad structure on $\FM_k(\C)$ and
its relation to OPE residues is Beilinson--Drinfeld
\cite{BD04}, Chapter~3.
Stage~$4$: the identification of $\barB(A)$ as an $\Eone$
dg coassociative coalgebra over $(\mathrm{ChirAss})^!$ is
Proposition~\ref{prop:bar-from-open}(iii).
Stage~$5$: the $\SCchtop$ structure on the pair
$(\cZ^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(A), A)$ is
Theorem~\ref{thm:universal-bulk}(ii).
Stage~$6$: the chiral endomorphism operad is
Definition~\ref{def:chiral-endomorphism-operad-v2}; the
$\Aut(\cO)$-equivariance is the coordinate-independence of
vertex algebra axioms (Frenkel--Ben-Zvi \cite{FBZ04},
Chapter~4).
Stage~$7$: the modular extension and curvature identity are
Volume~I, Theorem~D.

thinking
**Summarizing findings with precise references**

I’m gathering six concise bullet points linking exact line ranges in source files to specific outdated or incomplete claims about Theorems A, B, D, MC3, MC5, and topologization, focusing on the nuanced status of each and highlighting where claims go beyond what’s rigorously established. This will help deliver a sharp, line-numbered list pinpointing exactly what needs updating.
codex
- **Theorem A scope** – `chapters/connections/3d_gravity.tex:2160-2175`: the Steinberg remark still cites Vol I Theorem A as “Ω( B ) ≃ Vir” and “Ω(D_Ran(B)) ≃ Vir_{26-c}”, effectively treating the bar/cobar inversion as the theorem. The current Vol I convention is that Theorem A records the Verdier-dual algebra-level factorization (D_Ran( B ) is the homotopy Koszul dual) rather than a bare bar-level equivalence, so the citation here reads outdated and should emphasise the algebra-level Verdier statement.
- **Theorem B scope** – `chapters/theory/foundations_overclaims_resolved.tex:123-131`: the quoted argument says “on the Koszul locus, Theorem B gives a quasi-isomorphism Ω( B ) ≃ A,” but it still omits the Vol I coderived inversion clause that controls the non-Koszul locus. The current status bundle is “quasi-isomorphism on the Koszul locus, coderived inversion elsewhere,” so the text is missing the extra clause.
- **Theorem D scope** – `chapters/connections/concordance.tex:612-618`: the MC element remark claims “the genus tower is its ħ-expansion (Vol I, Theorem D),” which treats Theorem D as giving a blanket genus expansion. The updated status is “genus 1 unconditional, higher-genus only on proved lanes,” so this general “genus tower” citation is stale.
- **MC3 status** – `chapters/connections/holomorphic_topological.tex:1076-1088`: the comparison table entry “Yangian symmetry → ✔ MC3 (all simple types)” portrays the full MC3 programme as proved, whereas Vol I only establishes the evaluation-generated core (post-core/Baxter extension remains conditional). The table needs to flag that only the evaluation core/DK comparison is settled.
- **MC5 status** – `chapters/connections/twisted_holography_quantum_gravity.tex:2496-2504`: the Nishinaka–Vicedo remark says “the modular extension to all genera is proved in Vol I via MC5 (Theorem general-hs-sewing),” implying the entire modular/genus tower identification is settled. In fact, only the analytic HS-sewing lane and coderived BV=bar are proved; the genuswise chain-level BV/BRST/bar identification is still conjectural beyond that analytic lane, so the “all genera” phrasing is overstated.
- **Topologization scope** – `chapters/theory/foundations_recast_draft.tex:738-748`: Stage 9 currently states “[Vol I] Theorem topologization and Dunn decomposition are proved,” yet the modern status requires qualifiers (“proved for affine KM non-critical, DS/W, freely-generated PVA; otherwise the general topologization conjecture remains open”). The existing line still reads as if topologization is unconditional, so it should be scoped to the families where Vol I actually carries the proof.

chapters/connections/ht_bulk_boundary_line_core.tex:60:(Volume~I, Theorem~A identifies $\cA^!$ as the Koszul dual).
chapters/theory/introduction.tex:2064:The table distinguishes two Koszul duals. The \emph{chiral Koszul dual} $\cA^!_{\mathrm{ch}}$ (in the Francis--Gaitsgory sense) is the vertex algebra obtained by Verdier duality on the chiral symmetric bar coalgebra (Volume~I, Theorem~A: $D_{\mathrm{Ran}}(\barBch(\cA)) \simeq \barBch(\cA^!)$); for affine Kac--Moody, $\cA^!_{\mathrm{ch}} = \mathrm{CE}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\widehat{\fg}_{-k-2h^\vee})$ has the same modular characteristic as the Feigin--Frenkel dual level (the Koszul dual is the chiral CE algebra, not $\widehat{\fg}_{-k-2h^\vee}$ itself). The \emph{line-side Koszul dual} $\cA^!_{\mathrm{line}}$ is the $E_1$ Koszul dual obtained from the ordered bar $\barB^{\mathrm{ord}}(\cA)$; in the standard affine HT gauge realization, $\cA^!_{\mathrm{line}} = \Ydg(\fg)$, the dg-shifted Yangian identified by Theorem~\ref{thm:Koszul_dual_Yangian}. On the chirally Koszul locus, the line category is modeled by modules for $\cA^!_{\mathrm{line}}$ via Theorem~\ref{thm:lines_as_modules}. For the Heisenberg, $\cA^!_{\mathrm{ch}} = \mathrm{Sym}^{\mathrm{ch}}(V^*)$ (note $\cH_k^! \neq \cH_{-k}$: the chiral Koszul dual is the chiral symmetric algebra on the dual space, not the Heisenberg at negative level), while $\cA^!_{\mathrm{line}} = Y(\mathfrak{u}(1))$ (the abelian Yangian, with $\kappa = -k$) has modules forming the semisimple Fock-module line category. For Virasoro, the chiral dual is $\operatorname{Vir}_{26-c}$, while the matching line-side Virasoro-module picture is expected and used heuristically but is not isolated as a separate theorem on the live surface.

Contracting with OPE data: the square $\dfib^{\,2}$ of the
bar differential on a degree-$k$ bar element involves two
propagator insertions.  At the cogenerator projection, only
the self-contraction (tadpole) survives, extracting
$\kappa(\cA)$ (Volume~I, Theorem~D).  Therefore
$\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$.
\end{proof}

 succeeded in 52ms:
\item \textbf{Curvature at genus $g \geq 1$.}  At genus~$g \geq 1$,
  the curvature $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$ is
  the image of the annular trace under the clutching map:
  the cyclic trace on the open sector seeds the Calabi--Yau
  datum, and genus raising by clutching produces
  $\kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g \in H^0(\Mbar_g, \omega_{\Mbar_g})$.
  The bar differential inherits this as a curved structure
  (Volume~I, Theorem~D).  The bar complex becomes a
  \emph{curved factorization coalgebra}: a coalgebra in the
  coderived category of $D$-modules on $\Ran(\Sigma_g)$.

$|E(\Gamma)|$.

\emph{Base case}: $|E(\Gamma)|=0$, so $\Gamma$ has a single
vertex with $g(v) = g$ and no edges.  Then

complex alone (Volume~I, Theorem~D).

\medskip

The combinatorial depth of the self-intersection has a direct
arithmetic shadow. For an affine Kac--Moody algebra
$\widehat\fg_k$, the graded Euler characteristic of the ordered
bar complex satisfies
$\chi(\barB^{\mathrm{ord}}(\widehat\fg_k); q) = -1 +
\eta(q)^{\dim\fg}$, where
$\eta(q) = q^{1/24}\prod_{n \geq 1}(1-q^n)$ is the Dedekind eta
function. This is the bar-complex avatar of the denominator
identity: the combinatorial data of collisions on
$\FM_k(\C) \times \Conf_k^{<}(\R)$ reassemble into a modular form
of weight $(\dim\fg)/2$.

$\mathbb{C}_z \times I$, where $I = [0,1]$ is an interval with
a boundary condition $D$ on $\{0\}$ and a transverse boundary
condition $N$ on $\{1\}$. Each face supports a chiral algebra:
$\cA$ on the $D$-wall, $\cA^!$ on the $N$-wall
(Volume~I, Theorem~A identifies $\cA^!$ as the Koszul dual).
The slab $D \,|\, T \,|\, N$ has \emph{two} boundary
components \cite{Dimofte25}. It is a bimodule geometry, not a
Swiss-cheese disk: the Swiss-cheese operad $\SCchtop$ has one

 succeeded in 51ms:
\begin{remark}[Cohomological vs.\ cochain-level topologization]
\label{rem:cohomological-vs-cochain}
\index{topologization!cohomological vs cochain}
\index{class M@class~M!chain-level obstruction}
The proof of Construction~\ref{constr:topologization} is
\emph{cohomological}: it establishes local constancy on
$Q$-cohomology, not at the cochain level.
For class~L algebras (affine Kac--Moody), the
shadow tower is finite and chain-level and cohomological
data agree; the $\Ethree$-topological structure lifts to
cochains.
For class~M algebras (Virasoro, $\cW$-algebras), the
shadow tower is infinite: chain-level operations carry
essential information that vanishes on cohomology.
The $\Ethree$ upgrade may hold on cohomology but fail at the
cochain level. This is consistent with the broader pattern
that chain-level BV/bar identifications are proved for
classes~G and~L but remain conjectural for class~M.
Formality of the little $2$-disks operad
(Kontsevich~\cite{Kon03}, Tamarkin~\cite{Tamarkin00})
 succeeded in 50ms:

\emph{Lines (abstract).}
Theorem~\ref{thm:lines_as_modules} provides the abstract line-side
package: the open-colour $E_1$-module category associated to the
bar complex $\barB(\mathrm{Vir}_c)$. This exists for any
logarithmic $\mathrm{SC}^{\mathrm{ch,top}}$-algebra and requires
no additional input beyond homotopy-Koszulity of the operad
(Theorem~\ref{thm:homotopy-Koszul}).

The three faces close into the triangle
bulk $\xrightarrow{\beta_B}$ boundary $\xrightarrow{\barB}$
lines $\xrightarrow{\Zder}$ bulk, with the bulk-to-boundary map
given by the conformal Ward identity~\eqref{eq:gravity-bulk-boundary}.
\end{proof}

whether principal
\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:E3-topological-DS}\textup{)} or

**Considering patching files**

I’m thinking that perhaps I should require a notes entry, but it wasn't requested. I don’t want to revert any previous changes. I’m considering patching multiple smaller files, maybe four at a time. For the first batch, I’d include the introduction, preface, concordance, holomorphic topology, and conclusion. I’ll need to verify the exact lines in the current version and use a small patch method to apply my changes. It seems like a solid plan.

M /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -142,3 +142,3 @@
 
-The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: it is the scalar such that $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
+The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: unconditionally at genus~$1$, one has $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_1$, and on the proved scalar higher-genus lane this continues as $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.

+The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: unconditionally at genus~$1$, one has $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_1$, and on the proved scalar higher-genus lane this continues as $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
 
 \section*{The differential: holomorphic factorisation}

 (Volume~I, Theorem~C). Every theorem in both volumes is a shadow of
 this geometry. The bar-cobar adjunction (Theorem~A) is the

-formal Darboux isomorphism at the self-intersection; the modular
-characteristic $\kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$ (Theorem~D) is the
-curvature of the Lagrangian family over~$\Mbar_{g,n}$; the chiral
+formal Darboux isomorphism on the Koszul locus, with an off-locus
+coderived replacement; the modular characteristic
+$\kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_1$ and its proved scalar higher-genus
+continuation (Theorem~D) give the curvature of the Lagrangian family
+over~$\Mbar_{g,n}$; the chiral
 Hochschild ring (Theorem~H) is the ring of functions on the
 self-intersection. The five theorems are five faces of a single
 Lagrangian.

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index 50e6b1bc12f469a4cf3ac5b49e1555db8f82a7ac..e0c755ed56104e8c2219e3390b971c788fa0c484
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -140,7 +140,7 @@
 The holographic principle, on this surface, is the Darboux
 theorem.
 
-The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: it is the scalar such that $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
+The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: unconditionally at genus~$1$, one has $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_1$, and on the proved scalar higher-genus lane this continues as $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
 
 \section*{The differential: holomorphic factorisation}

 Lagrangian.

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index 50e6b1bc12f469a4cf3ac5b49e1555db8f82a7ac..e0c755ed56104e8c2219e3390b971c788fa0c484
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -140,7 +140,7 @@
 The holographic principle, on this surface, is the Darboux
 theorem.
 
-The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: it is the scalar such that $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
+The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: unconditionally at genus~$1$, one has $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_1$, and on the proved scalar higher-genus lane this continues as $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
 
 \section*{The differential: holomorphic factorisation}

+The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: unconditionally at genus~$1$, one has $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_1$, and on the proved scalar higher-genus lane this continues as $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
 
 \section*{The differential: holomorphic factorisation}

We establish the universal equivalence
$(i) \Leftrightarrow (ii)$, with
$(ii) \Leftrightarrow (iii)$ for
the affine specialisation.

\smallskip\noindent
\emph{$(i) \Leftrightarrow (ii)$:} This is the content of
Theorem~D (Volume~I): the curvature of the genus-$g$ bar complex
is $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$
is the fundamental class of the Hodge bundle on $\Mbar_g$. At
$g = 1$, $\omega_1 \in H^0(\Mbar_{1,1})$ is the fundamental class
(nonzero), so $\dfib^{\,2} = 0$ if and only if
$\kappa(\cA) = 0$.

\smallskip\noindent
\emph{$(ii) \Leftrightarrow (iii)$ in the affine lineage:}
 succeeded in 52ms:
The genus tower of any logarithmic $\SCchtop$-algebra is governed
by a \emph{single scalar} $\kappa(\cA)$:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
\item \textbf{No secondary curvature exists.}
 The curvature of the genus-$g$ bar complex is
 $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$ for all $g \geq 1$,
 with $\kappa(\cA) \in \mathbb{k}$ the modular characteristic and
 $\omega_g$ the fundamental class of the Hodge bundle
 \textup{(}Vol~I, Theorem~D\textup{)}.
 There is no independent secondary curvature $\kappa_2$ at
 genus~$2$: the same scalar $\kappa$ governs every genus.
 ``Type~H'' in the na\"ive sense of $\kappa = 0$ with
 $\kappa_2 \neq 0$ is therefore vacuous.
\item \textbf{Three regimes of uncurved algebras.}
 The algebras with $\kappa(\cA) = 0$ fall into three regimes:

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1346,1351p' chapters/theory/factorization_swiss_cheese.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '2057,2062p' chapters/theory/factorization_swiss_cheese.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '2798,2814p' chapters/connections/hochschild.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar-vol2 succeeded in 51ms:
\item \textbf{Curvature at genus $g \geq 1$.}  At genus~$g \geq 1$,
  the curvature $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$ is
  the image of the annular trace under the clutching map:
  the cyclic trace on the open sector seeds the Calabi--Yau
  datum, and genus raising by clutching produces
  $\kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g \in H^0(\Mbar_g, \omega_{\Mbar_g})$.
  The bar differential inherits this as a curved structure
  (Volume~I, Theorem~D).  The bar complex becomes a
  \emph{curved factorization coalgebra}: a coalgebra in the
  coderived category of $D$-modules on $\Ran(\Sigma_g)$.
 succeeded in 51ms:
bar differential on a degree-$k$ bar element involves two
propagator insertions.  At the cogenerator projection, only
the self-contraction (tadpole) survives, extracting
$\kappa(\cA)$ (Volume~I, Theorem~D).  Therefore
$\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$.
\end{proof}
 succeeded in 51ms:
\end{itemize}
That models~(i) and~(ii) have $d^2 = 0$ while model~(iii)
has $\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$ is the content of
Volume~I, Convention~3.1 and Theorem~D.  The derived-coderived
comparison between models~(i)/(ii) and model~(iii) is developed
later in the chapter; Theorem~\ref{thm:factorization-SC-koszul}(iv)
 succeeded in 50ms:
\index{Arakelov form!nodal degeneration}%
On the smooth locus of $\overline{\cM}_{g,n}$
(the open stratum $\cM_{g,n}$), the bar differential
$d^{(g)}$ on the genus-$g$ bar complex
$\barB^{(g)}(\cA)$ satisfies
$(d^{(g)})^2 = \kappa(\cA)\cdot\omega_g$, where the curvature
arises from the $\bar\partial$-defect of the Arakelov propagator
\textup{(}Volume~I, Theorem~D\textup{)}.  The restriction to
the boundary stratum indexed by $\Gamma$ satisfies:
\begin{equation}\label{eq:nodal-curvature-decomposition}
  \dfib^{\,2}\big|_\Gamma
  \;=\;
  \sum_{v\in V(\Gamma)}
  \kappa(\cA)\cdot\omega_{g(v)}
  \;+\;
  \sum_{e\in E(\Gamma)}
  \kappa(\cA)\cdot\delta_e,

\emph{Base case}: $|E(\Gamma)|=0$, so $\Gamma$ has a single
vertex with $g(v) = g$ and no edges.  Then
$B_\Gamma(\cA) = B^{(g)}_{n}(\cA)$ and the curvature is
$\dfib^{\,2} = \kappa(\cA)\cdot\omega_g$ from the vertex bar
complex alone (Volume~I, Theorem~D).

\emph{Inductive step}: suppose the decomposition holds for all
stable graphs with $<|E(\Gamma)|$ edges.  Adding the edge~$e$
that connects (or self-connects) vertex components introduces

+\ref{thm:E3-topological-DS-general},
+\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}\textup{)}; a general
+chain-level lift remains conjectural
 \textup{(}Conjecture~\ref{conj:E3-topological-general}\textup{)}.
 Without the conformal vector, the bulk depends on the complex
 structure of~$X$ and one has a holomorphic-topological theory

 (Volume~I, Theorem~C). Every theorem in both volumes is a shadow of
 this geometry. The bar-cobar adjunction (Theorem~A) is the
 Lagrangian neighborhood theorem; Koszul inversion (Theorem~B) is the
-formal Darboux isomorphism at the self-intersection; the modular

-curvature of the Lagrangian family over~$\Mbar_{g,n}$; the chiral
+formal Darboux isomorphism on the Koszul locus, with an off-locus
+coderived replacement; the modular characteristic
+$\kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_1$ and its proved scalar higher-genus
+continuation (Theorem~D) give the curvature of the Lagrangian family
+over~$\Mbar_{g,n}$; the chiral
 Hochschild ring (Theorem~H) is the ring of functions on the
 self-intersection. The five theorems are five faces of a single
 Lagrangian.

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index 50e6b1bc12f469a4cf3ac5b49e1555db8f82a7ac..e0c755ed56104e8c2219e3390b971c788fa0c484
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -140,7 +140,7 @@
 The holographic principle, on this surface, is the Darboux
 theorem.
 
-The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: it is the scalar such that $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
+The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: unconditionally at genus~$1$, one has $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_1$, and on the proved scalar higher-genus lane this continues as $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
 
 \section*{The differential: holomorphic factorisation}

The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: it is the scalar such that $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
+The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: unconditionally at genus~$1$, one has $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_1$, and on the proved scalar higher-genus lane this continues as $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
 
 \section*{The differential: holomorphic factorisation}

+\ref{thm:E3-topological-DS-general},
+\ref{thm:E3-topological-free-PVA}\textup{)}; a general
+chain-level lift remains conjectural
 \textup{(}Conjecture~\ref{conj:E3-topological-general}\textup{)}.
 Without the conformal vector, the bulk depends on the complex
 structure of~$X$ and one has a holomorphic-topological theory

 (Volume~I, Theorem~C). Every theorem in both volumes is a shadow of

+over~$\Mbar_{g,n}$; the chiral
 Hochschild ring (Theorem~H) is the ring of functions on the
 self-intersection. The five theorems are five faces of a single
 Lagrangian.

  $\kappa_2 \neq 0$ is therefore vacuous.

diff --git a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
index 50e6b1bc12f469a4cf3ac5b49e1555db8f82a7ac..e0c755ed56104e8c2219e3390b971c788fa0c484
--- a/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/introduction.tex
@@ -140,7 +140,7 @@
 The holographic principle, on this surface, is the Darboux
 theorem.
 
-The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: it is the scalar such that $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
+The $(-2)$-shifted symplectic geometry of the formal neighborhood is governed by three representation-theoretic invariants computed in Volume~I. The modular characteristic $\kappa(\cA)$ controls the curvature of the Lagrangian embedding: unconditionally at genus~$1$, one has $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_1$, and on the proved scalar higher-genus lane this continues as $d_{\barB}^2 = \kappa \cdot \omega_g$, where $\omega_g$ is the Hodge class on $\overline{\cM}_g$. The complementarity theorem (Volume~I, Theorem~C) lifts to the bulk-boundary-line triangle: the decomposition $Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!) = H^*(\overline{\cM}_g, Z(\cA))$ becomes a Lagrangian splitting of the self-intersection complex, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses (satisfied for all standard families; conditional in general). Three structure theorems from Volume~I govern the formal neighborhood. \emph{Algebraicity} (the Riccati theorem: $H(t)^2 = t^4 Q_L(t)$, with $Q_L$ quadratic) determines the growth rate $\rho$ of the shadow obstruction tower and hence the convergence of the genus expansion. \emph{Formality identification} (the shadow obstruction tower equals the $L_\infty$ formality obstruction tower at all degrees, proved by induction on~$r$ in Volume~I) explains why the Lagrangian extension terminates for some algebras and accumulates infinitely for others: tower termination is $L_\infty$ formality. \emph{Complementarity} lifts to the holomorphic-topological split: the $(-1)$-shifted symplectic structure on the self-intersection complex $C_g(\cA)$ (inherited from the $(-2)$-shifted ambient stack) is the geometric incarnation of the Lagrangian decomposition $C_g(\cA) = Q_g(\cA) + Q_g(\cA^!)$, under perfectness and chain-level nondegeneracy hypotheses satisfied by all standard families. The shadow depth classification $\mathbf{G}/\mathbf{L}/\mathbf{C}/\mathbf{M}$ of Volume~I becomes a classification of bulk-boundary pairs by the critical discriminant $\Delta = 8\kappa S_4$. Class~$\mathbf{G}$ ($\Delta = 0$) is formal: the bulk is determined classically, the Lagrangian self-intersection is clean, and no higher $\Ainf$ operations survive. Classes $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{C}$, and $\mathbf{M}$ ($\Delta \neq 0$) are genuinely curved: the self-intersection carries excess Tor, and the higher $\Ainf$ operations encode the successive obstruction classes. The boundary algebra $A_b$ is recovered from the genus-$0$ closed data of the universal MC element~$\Theta_\cA$ from Volume~I, while the higher-genus shadow data descend through the shadow obstruction tower.
 
 \section*{The differential: holomorphic factorisation}
